content.content:
1→//
     2→//  StoreKitConstants.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→
     6→import Foundation
     7→
     8→enum StoreKitProductID {
     9→    // MARK: - Pro Tier
    10→    static let proMonthly = "com.fittoday.pro.monthly"
    11→    static let proAnnual = "com.fittoday.pro.annual"
    12→
    13→    // MARK: - Elite Tier
    14→    static let eliteMonthly = "com.fittoday.elite.monthly"
    15→    static let eliteAnnual = "com.fittoday.elite.annual"
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - Sets
    18→    static let proProducts: Set<String> = [proMonthly, proAnnual]
    19→    static let eliteProducts: Set<String> = [eliteMonthly, eliteAnnual]
    20→    static let allProducts: Set<String> = proProducts.union(eliteProducts)
    21→
    22→    // MARK: - Tier Resolution
    23→    static func tier(for productID: String) -> SubscriptionTier {
    24→        if eliteProducts.contains(productID) { return .elite }
    25→        if proProducts.contains(productID) { return .pro }
    26→        return .free
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    static func isAnnual(_ productID: String) -> Bool {
    30→        productID == proAnnual || productID == eliteAnnual
    31→    }
    32→}
    33→

content.text:
Tudo alinhado. Aqui o resumo da verificação:

### Product IDs - OK

| Produto | App Store Connect | StoreKit Config | Código |
|---------|------------------|-----------------|--------|
| Pro Mensal | `com.fittoday.pro.monthly` | `com.fittoday.pro.monthly` | `com.fittoday.pro.monthly` |
| Pro Anual | `com.fittoday.pro.annual` | `com.fittoday.pro.annual` | `com.fittoday.pro.annual` |
| Elite Mensal | `com.fittoday.elite.monthly` | `com.fittoday.elite.monthly` | `com.fittoday.elite.monthly` |
| Elite Anual | `com.fittoday.elite.annual` | `com.fittoday.elite.annual` | `com.fittoday.elite.annual` |

### Um ponto de atenção: Subscription Group

No **App Store Connect** o grupo se chama **"FitToday Premium"** (todos os 4 produtos no mesmo grupo). No **StoreKit config** você tem **2 grupos separados**: "FitToday Pro" e "FitToday Elite".

No App Store Connect, ter todos no mesmo grupo é o correto — permite upgrade/downgrade entre Pro e Elite. Mas o StoreKit config local com 2 grupos separados pode causar comportamento diferente nos testes locais vs produção.

Quer que eu unifique os subscription groups no `Configuration.storekit` para um único grupo "FitToday Premium" para espelhar o App Store Connect?

content.text: Good — "FitToday Pro" and "FitToday Elite" in `RevenueCatEntitlementRepository` are **entitlement names**, not subscription group names, so those are correct and separate from the StoreKit group config. Now let me create the branch and fix the StoreKit config.

Now I'll unify the two subscription groups into one "FitToday Premium" group, keeping the correct `groupLevel` ordering (Elite products at higher levels than Pro, matching App Store Connect where level 1 = highest tier):

content.text: Now commit and create the PR.

input.command:
git add FitToday/FitToday/Configuration.storekit && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: unify StoreKit subscription groups to match App Store Connect

The local StoreKit config had two separate subscription groups
("FitToday Pro" and "FitToday Elite") while App Store Connect uses
a single "FitToday Premium" group. This mismatch caused different
upgrade/downgrade behavior in local testing vs production/RevenueCat.

Merged all 4 products into one group with correct level ordering:
1 - Elite Anual, 2 - Elite Mensal, 3 - Pro Anual, 4 - Pro Mensal
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: unify StoreKit subscription groups to match App Store Connect" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Merged two separate StoreKit subscription groups ("FitToday Pro" and "FitToday Elite") into a single "FitToday Premium" group
- Matches the App Store Connect configuration where all 4 products live in one group
- Ensures local StoreKit testing reflects the same upgrade/downgrade behavior as production and RevenueCat

## Changes
- `Configuration.storekit`: Unified subscription groups with correct level ordering:
  - Level 1: Elite Anual (highest tier)
  - Level 2: Elite Mensal
  - Level 3: Pro Anual
  - Level 4: Pro Mensal (lowest tier)

## Test plan
- [ ] Build succeeds with updated StoreKit config
- [ ] StoreKit testing sandbox shows all 4 products in one group
- [ ] Upgrade from Pro → Elite works correctly in sandbox
- [ ] Downgrade from Elite → Pro works correctly in sandbox
- [ ] RevenueCat dashboard reflects correct subscription group

Closes PRO-103
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR criado: https://github.com/Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday/pull/111

**Resumo da mudança:**
- Unifiquei os 2 grupos separados ("FitToday Pro" + "FitToday Elite") em um único grupo **"FitToday Premium"** no `Configuration.storekit`
- Ordem dos níveis espelha o App Store Connect: Elite Anual (1) > Elite Mensal (2) > Pro Anual (3) > Pro Mensal (4)
- Todos os 4 produtos agora compartilham o mesmo `subscriptionGroupID`, permitindo upgrade/downgrade correto nos testes locais — igual ao comportamento do RevenueCat em produção
